pages.full_text:
[Hand-drawn plan/sketch of Skenfrith Castle — see route_sketch_description]

__Skenfrith Castle.__
One of 3 built by Normans in wood before 1100. Rebuilt 1270 in stone (along with Tretower) subduing Gwent.
  * central tower had appartments on first floor. Archers on roof shooting over outer walls.
pages.route_sketch_description: Plan-view diagram of Skenfrith Castle. Outer curtain wall (roughly square with rounded corners) contains: 'Corner Tower' (circle, top-left), unlabelled circular tower (top-right), two more rounded corner bastions (bottom-left and bottom-right). Along the top inner wall: 'wooden buildings'. Centre of courtyard: 'Tower' with asterisk (the circular keep). Left wall: 'watergate' (rectangular feature). Right inner area: 'C17 stone building'. Centre-lower area: 'oven + kitchens' (circle) and 'Chapel'. Bottom gate: 'gate + drawbridge' with arrow pointing outward. Below outer wall: 'mill.' label with upward arrow. Top of diagram: 'river' label with arrow. Curtain wall and features drawn in blue pen.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. The sketch labels include 'C17 stone building' (likely 17th century). 'appartments' is the diary's spelling. Year not determinable from this page alone; context from adjacent pages would be needed.

pages.full_text:
After tea we drove on the Malvern & had pizza before listening to Peter play in the concerto concerto at Malvern, led by Thomas. Then we went home.
  * After lunch we drove back to __Capel-y-ffin__, where there was supposed to be a service in a tiny church at 2.30, but the service had been cancelled. It was a pretty church. The graveyard had an inscription on the back of a headstone "Better to smoke here than in the hereafter". Next to it were 2 small headstones which had been carved by Eric Gill. Simon telephoned the vicar to complain about the misleading notice.
__September 22nd.__
  There has [strikethrough: been] been a huge gap because we hired an RV in California for a month. The apples, tomatoes & bananas that we left in the van at the beginning of July were still recognisable today.
  Charles, Simon & Eve, + Tonic are going
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '2' visible in top-right corner — this may be part of a larger number (e.g. 12, 22, 52 etc.) but only '2' is clearly legible at the outer corner. Year not stated on this page; the text mentions hiring an RV in California from early July through to September 22nd, which could place this in the 1990s given the volume range (1989-2003). 'concerto concerto' appears to be a repetition in the original. 'appartments' and other spellings preserved verbatim. There is a strikethrough over 'been' where it appears written twice (corrected in the same line). The name 'Tonic' appears as a superscript addition between 'Eve,' and 'are going'. Text cuts off at bottom of page.
